19743294.animalDescription: Fizz & Flurry are beautiful bonded sisters who are on the shy side, especially in the scary shelter environment, but sweet in calm surroundings where they feel safe. Dilute tortie girl Flurry may hiss when she’s scared, but turns on her purr motor with pets and enjoys being held. Black-and-white girl Fizz adores leaping after wand toys and also snuggling up on a warm lap for pets. She can get a little overexcited at times, and will do well with a guardian who can help her redirect that energy away from human hands and fingers.

22316191.animalDescription:
The rain hadn’t started yet, but the clouds were thinking about it—the kind of gray afternoon when a small, sharp-eyed gumshoe keeps to the shadows. That’s where I first noticed Philip Marlowe. Black and white like a tuxedo at a low-rent casino, quiet as a secret, and twice as careful with who he trusts.

Marlowe’s a young cat with the soul of a private detective: observant, polite, and a little shy until he knows the room is safe. He doesn’t crash parties and he doesn’t chase trouble. Give him a calm place, a soft chair, and a human who understands the language of cats, and he’ll settle in like he’s finally closed a tough case.

He’s looking for a quiet home with cat-savvy adopters—no kids, no dogs, no funny business. Just good company, steady routines, and someone who doesn’t mind earning a detective’s trust the old-fashioned way.

There’s a nice twist in the file, too: His adoption fee is waived if you flash your Oakland Public Library card. Seems Marlowe’s got a soft spot for readers.

If you want to meet this soft-spoken sleuth, you can find him here Thursday from noon to 6:30 or Friday through Sunday, noon to 3. Stop by, tip your hat, and see if this tuxedo detective might be the partner your home has been missing.
22316191.animalSummary: I am at Oakland Animal Services in kennel Cat Holding 4:5

22316193.animalDescription:
Agatha Christie is a young lady of quiet habits and impeccable whiskers—a 1-year-old ticked brown tabby whose story begins, as many good mysteries do, with a touch of reserve. At first meeting she may observe you thoughtfully, eyes taking in every detail like a seasoned detective sizing up a suspect.

But offer a gentle hand and a few soft pets, and the case quickly unfolds. Miss Christie warms to kind company with surprising speed, leaning in closer, her purr rising like a well-timed plot twist. Before long, she’s quite certain you’re no villain at all—merely the hero of her next chapter.

Prospective companions may make her acquaintance Thursdays from noon to 6:30 p.m., or Friday through Sunday from noon to 3. And here’s a most delightful clue: Her adoption fee is entirely waived for visitors who present their Oakland Public Library card.

If you’re seeking a clever little companion with a fondness for quiet affection and a talent for winning hearts, Agatha Christie may be ready to close the case—and move into your home for good.
